text.title: Edit profile
text.description: Edit your profile by changing the information below.

codeConnect.label: React
codeConnect.snippet: <Modal open type="transactional" title="Edit profile" subTitle="Edit your profile by changing the information below.">{children}</Modal>

ignoredDesignFields.reason: Design sample content only. Do not hard-code as the runtime Input value.
Design sample content only. Do not hard-code as the runtime Input value.

pattern.description: A modal containing a short description, form fields, and footer actions.

rules: Use the mapped modal node as the top-level component.
Use fixture labels for Input labels.
Treat fixture field values as design sample content, not runtime Input values.
Use secondary actions before primary actions.

generationRules: Use only props listed in the component contracts.
Do not hard-code design sample field values into Input value props.
Do not pass raw style objects, className, css, sx, or raw color props.
Represent layout through component structure instead of pixel-perfect CSS.
Run validate_generated_code before presenting generated React as usable.

generationPolicy: AI-generated component usage should prefer semantic color roles through component props and theme.hugoUIColorRoles.
Do not hardcode raw palette values in generated component usage.
Use raw palette names only when documenting or updating the design-token layer itself.

components.needsReviewReason: First contract version includes component-authored props plus selected inherited props that are used by source, stories, or tests. Review before treating all inherited MUI escape hatches as covered.
First contract version records compatibility props and selected TextField inherited props, but does not attempt to recommend every inherited MUI TextField option.
First contract version captures Modal and ModalFooter public structures, but inherited Dialog props are intentionally not exhaustively recommended.

notes: This manifest covers only the MUI package scope.
Use the component contract files as the machine-readable entry points for AI generation and validation.

componentContracts.description: Button contract adapted from @hugo-ui/mui AI contract artifact.

colorTheme.description: The color theme of the button

loading.description: Indicates whether the button is loading

loadingPosition.description: The loading indicator can be positioned on the start or the center of the button.

labelHidden.description: Indicates whether the label is hidden; used to save the space on smaller screens

fullWidth.description: If `true`, the button will take up the full width of its container.

type.description:
The HTML [`type`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/button#type)
attribute applied to `button` and `a` elements.
Ignored when rendering non-native buttons.

href.description:
The URL to link to when the button is clicked.
If defined, an `a` element will be used as the root node.

sx.description: The system prop that allows defining system overrides as well as additional CSS styles.

component.description: Inherited MUI root element override. Do not use by default in AI-generated Button usage.

loading.notes: loading disables click behavior; center loading preserves button width.

iconOnly.notes: Use only when children provides an accessible label and an icon is visible.

discouragedProps.name: sx
discouragedProps.reason: Public MUI escape hatch. AI-generated usage should prefer level, colorTheme, and size before raw style overrides.

discouragedProps.name: style
discouragedProps.reason: Public React escape hatch. Use only for layout integration that cannot be expressed by component props.

discouragedProps.name: className
discouragedProps.reason: Public styling escape hatch. Do not use to recreate internal HugoUIButton classes.

discouragedProps.name: component
discouragedProps.reason: Inherited MUI root element escape hatch. AI-generated usage should keep the default Button root unless integration explicitly requires an override.

generationRules.rule: Default generated Button usage should use level="primary", colorTheme="purple", size="medium", and type="button" unless the design indicates otherwise.
Use colorTheme="red" with the primary level for destructive actions.
Use startIcon or endIcon only when the icon adds meaning or improves recognition; keep visible text unless labelHidden is required.

validationRules.rule: Generated code must import Button from @hugo-ui/mui.
Generated code must not use MUI color or variant props on Button.

generationPolicy: Do not hardcode raw color values for normal button usage.
Choose colorTheme and level instead of overriding MUI color or variant.
Use colorTheme="red" only for destructive primary actions.

props.description: The content of the component.

props.description: The level of the button

props.description: The color theme of the button

props.description: Indicates whether the button is loading

props.description: The loading indicator can be positioned on the start or the center of the button.

props.name: disabled
props.description: If `true`, the component is disabled.

props.name: startIcon
props.description: Element placed before the children.

props.name: endIcon
props.description: Element placed after the children.

props.name: fullWidth
props.description: If `true`, the button will take up the full width of its container.

props.name: type
props.description:
The HTML [`type`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/button#type)
attribute applied to `button` and `a` elements.
Ignored when rendering non-native buttons.

forbiddenProps.name: color
forbiddenProps.reason: HugoUIButtonCommonProps omits MUI ButtonProps color. Use colorTheme instead.

forbiddenProps.name: variant
forbiddenProps.reason: HugoUIButtonCommonProps omits MUI ButtonProps variant. Use level instead.

discouragedProps.name: sx
discouragedProps.reason: Public MUI escape hatch. AI-generated usage should prefer level, colorTheme, and size before raw style overrides.

discouragedProps.name: style
discouragedProps.reason: Public React escape hatch. Use only for layout integration that cannot be expressed by component props.

discouragedProps.name: className
discouragedProps.reason: Public styling escape hatch. Do not use to recreate internal HugoUIButton classes.

discouragedProps.name: component
discouragedProps.reason: Inherited MUI root element escape hatch. AI-generated usage should keep the default Button root unless integration explicitly requires an override.

loading.notes: loading disables click behavior; center loading preserves button width.

iconOnly.notes: Use only when children provides an accessible label and an icon is visible.

generationPolicy: Do not hardcode raw color values for normal button usage.
Choose colorTheme and level instead of overriding MUI color or variant.
Use colorTheme="red" only for destructive primary actions.

a11yRules.rule: When loading is true, the component sets aria-busy and prevents click handling.
When labelHidden is true, children must be a meaningful accessible label.
Use type="button" unless the button is intentionally submitting or resetting a form.

generationRules.rule: Default generated Button usage should use level="primary", colorTheme="purple", size="medium", and type="button" unless the design indicates otherwise.
Use colorTheme="red" with the primary level for destructive actions.
Use startIcon or endIcon only when the icon adds meaning or improves recognition; keep visible text unless labelHidden is required.

validationRules.rule: Generated code must import Button from @hugo-ui/mui.
Generated code must not use MUI color or variant props on Button.

rawContract.needsReviewReason: First contract version includes component-authored props plus selected inherited props that are used by source, stories, or tests. Review before treating all inherited MUI escape hatches as covered.

componentContracts.description: Input contract adapted from @hugo-ui/mui AI contract artifact.

id.description:
The id of the `input` element.
Use this prop to make `label` and `helperText` accessible for screen readers.

value.description: The value of the `input` element, required for a controlled component.

extraMessage.description:
Used for the success/error message

Should only be used with `color="success"` or `color="error"`

Type: `node` (supports `React.ReactNode`)

color.description: Status color for validation feedback. AI-generated usage should leave this unset by default and use success or error only with matching extraMessage.

showCount.description: Only used for `multiline`. To show/hide the word count below the field

multiline.description: If `true`, a `textarea` element is rendered instead of an input.

required.description: If `true`, the label is displayed as required and the `input` element is required.

fullWidth.description: If `true`, the input will take up the full width of its container.

icon.description: Used for customizing the postfix icon.

autoFocus.description: If `true`, the `input` element is focused during the first mount.

autoFocusByKeyboard.description: If `true`, when `autoFocus` is true, it should be focus visible status

type.description: Type of the `input` element. It should be [a valid HTML5 input type](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/input#input_types).

onChange.description: Callback fired when the value is changed.

InputProps.description: MUI v5 compatibility prop. Prefer `slotProps.input` for new code.

inputProps.description: MUI v5 compatibility prop. Prefer `slotProps.htmlInput` for new code.

InputLabelProps.description: MUI v5 compatibility prop. Prefer `slotProps.inputLabel` for new code.

slotProps.description: The props used for each slot inside.

inputRef.description: Pass a ref to the `input` element.

sx.description: The system prop that allows defining system overrides as well as additional CSS styles.

label.notes: Visible label for normal inputs; search inputs may use the label as placeholder.

values.success: Success status with InputStatus rendering for string extraMessage
values.error: Error status with InputStatus rendering for string extraMessage

multilineCount.notes: showCount applies to multiline inputs and uses inputProps.maxLength or the default max length.

loading.notes: Shows an end adornment loading indicator and sets aria-busy on the HTML input.

discouragedProps.name: InputProps
discouragedProps.reason: MUI v5 compatibility escape hatch. Prefer slotProps.input for new code.

discouragedProps.name: inputProps
discouragedProps.reason: MUI v5 compatibility escape hatch. Prefer slotProps.htmlInput for new code.

discouragedProps.name: InputLabelProps
discouragedProps.reason: MUI v5 compatibility escape hatch. Prefer slotProps.inputLabel for new code.

discouragedProps.name: sx
discouragedProps.reason: Inherited MUI styling escape hatch. Prefer component props and theme tokens.

discouragedProps.name: style
discouragedProps.reason: Use only for local layout integration.

discouragedProps.name: className
discouragedProps.reason: Public styling escape hatch. Do not use to recreate internal HugoUIInput classes.

conditionalProps.name: color
conditionalProps.description: Status color for validation feedback. AI-generated usage should leave this unset by default and use success or error only with matching extraMessage.

generationRules.rule: Use value and onChange together for controlled examples.
Use extraMessage with color="success" or color="error" when displaying validation feedback.
Do not choose InputProps, inputProps, or InputLabelProps by default; they are compatibility escape hatches.

validationRules.rule: Generated code must import Input from @hugo-ui/mui.
If color is success or error, prefer a matching extraMessage.

generationPolicy: Use color="success" or color="error" only when extraMessage communicates that state.
Do not hardcode border, focus, success, or error colors.
Prefer slotProps over compatibility props for new generated code.

discouragedProps.name: style

label.notes: Visible label for normal inputs; search inputs may use the label as placeholder.

values.success: Success status with InputStatus rendering for string extraMessage
values.error: Error status with InputStatus rendering for string extraMessage

multilineCount.notes: showCount applies to multiline inputs and uses inputProps.maxLength or the default max length.

loading.notes: Shows an end adornment loading indicator and sets aria-busy on the HTML input.

generationPolicy: Use color="success" or color="error" only when extraMessage communicates that state.
Do not hardcode border, focus, success, or error colors.
Prefer slotProps over compatibility props for new generated code.

a11yRules.rule: Provide label or an explicit aria label through html input props for generated inputs.
When required is true, the component marks the input aria-required and renders a visual required postfix.
When color is error, the component sets aria-invalid on the HTML input.
For single-line inputs, Enter triggers the onBlur callback; multiline inputs preserve Enter for text entry.

generationRules.rule: Use value and onChange together for controlled examples.
Use extraMessage with color="success" or color="error" when displaying validation feedback.
Do not choose InputProps, inputProps, or InputLabelProps by default; they are compatibility escape hatches.

validationRules.rule: Generated code must import Input from @hugo-ui/mui.
If color is success or error, prefer a matching extraMessage.

rawContract.needsReviewReason: First contract version records compatibility props and selected TextField inherited props, but does not attempt to recommend every inherited MUI TextField option.

componentContracts.description: Modal contract adapted from @hugo-ui/mui AI contract artifact.

type.description: Visual and behavioral intent of the modal.

onClose.description: The function to be called when the modal is closed

buttonDefs.description: The button definitions for the modal

closeButton.description: Controls whether the modal title renders a close button. Error, informational, and feedback modals are closeable by default.

footerComponent.description: The footer component of the modal

headerComponent.description: The header component of the modal

loading.description: Indicates whether the modal is loading

headerPrefixIcon.description: allow user to customize their prefix icon of Modal header

disableAutoFocus.description: allows user to disable the auto focus onto the bottom action buttons in the case there is another element to focus on first

className.description: The class name of the component

slots.description: The components used for each slot inside.

transitionDuration.description:
The duration for the transition, in milliseconds.
You may specify a single timeout for all transitions, or individually with an object.

actions.notes: Primary and secondary render HugoUIButton. Tertiary renders Link unless renderCustomComponent is supplied.

feedback.notes: messages is used when type is feedback and supports success, destructiveSuccess, and error message types.

customRegions.notes: Use only when the standard title and footer do not match the design.

loading.notes: loading constrains action buttons; showLoadingIndicator controls panel-level loading feedback.

discouragedProps.name: slots
discouragedProps.reason: Inherited Dialog escape hatch. Use only when replacing MUI internals is required.

discouragedProps.name: style
discouragedProps.reason: Use only for local layout integration.

discouragedProps.name: className
discouragedProps.reason: Do not use to recreate internal HugoUIModal classes.

generationRules.rule: Default generated Modal usage should use type="transactional", a controlled open prop, onClose, title, children, and buttonDefs.primary.
Use buttonDefs.primary for the main action, buttonDefs.secondary for cancel/back, and buttonDefs.tertiary for low-emphasis link actions.
For completion messages, use type="feedback" with messages instead of manually composing a transactional modal.

validationRules.rule: Generated code must import Modal from @hugo-ui/mui.
Generated code must pass open explicitly.
buttonDefs may contain primary, secondary, and tertiary entries matching the ModalFooter public types.

generationPolicy: Use type to choose visual intent instead of overriding modal colors.
Do not hardcode header, icon, footer, focus, warning, or error colors.
Use buttonDefs to customize modal actions rather than replacing the footer by default.

props.name: open
props.description: If `true`, the component is shown.

props.name: title
props.description: The title of the modal

props.name: type
props.description: Visual and behavioral intent of the modal.

props.name: onClose
props.description: The function to be called when the modal is closed

props.name: buttonDefs
props.description: The button definitions for the modal

props.name: children
props.description: The content of the modal

props.name: closeButton
props.description: Controls whether the modal title renders a close button. Error, informational, and feedback modals are closeable by default.

props.name: footerComponent
props.description: The footer component of the modal

props.name: headerComponent
props.description: The header component of the modal

props.name: loading
props.description: Indicates whether the modal is loading

props.name: showLoadingIndicator
props.description: show loading indicator on Modal

props.name: messages
props.description: only for feedback

props.name: headerPrefixIcon
props.description: allow user to customize their prefix icon of Modal header

props.name: disableAutoFocus
props.description: allows user to disable the auto focus onto the bottom action buttons in the case there is another element to focus on first

props.name: id
props.description: The id of the component

props.name: className
props.description: The class name of the component

props.name: style
props.description: The style of the component

props.name: slots
props.description: The components used for each slot inside.

props.name: transitionDuration
props.description:
The duration for the transition, in milliseconds.
You may specify a single timeout for all transitions, or individually with an object.

props.name: fullScreen
props.description: If `true`, the dialog is full-screen.

properties.name: primary
properties.description: The primary button (save button)

properties.name: secondary
properties.description: The secondary button (cancel button)

properties.name: tertiary
properties.description: The tertiary button (link button)

properties.name: level
properties.description: The level of the button

properties.name: colorTheme
properties.description: The color theme of the button

properties.name: size
properties.description: The size of the button

properties.name: loading
properties.description: Indicates whether the button is loading

properties.name: loadingPosition
properties.description: The loading indicator can be positioned on the start or the center of the button.

properties.name: labelHidden
properties.description: Indicates whether the label is hidden; used to save the space on smaller screens

properties.name: level
properties.description: The level of the button

properties.name: colorTheme
properties.description: The color theme of the button

properties.name: size
properties.description: The size of the button

properties.name: loading
properties.description: Indicates whether the button is loading

properties.name: loadingPosition
properties.description: The loading indicator can be positioned on the start or the center of the button.

properties.name: labelHidden
properties.description: Indicates whether the label is hidden; used to save the space on smaller screens

properties.name: children
properties.description: The content of the link

properties.name: className
properties.description: The CSS class name of the root element.

properties.name: mode
properties.description: The color scheme of the link text; `white`, `light`, and `dark` are all the colors of the background

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Indicates whether the link is disabled

properties.name: size
properties.description: The size of the link

properties.name: loading
properties.description: Indicates whether the link is loading/busy

properties.name: component
properties.description: Allows uses to change the element type of the link; default is `a`

properties.name: type
properties.description: The type of the message

properties.name: message
properties.description: The message to display

properties.name: description
properties.description: The description of the message

properties.name: descriptonComponentType
properties.description: default component type is p, if the description is more complex component, allow user to change the component type

examples.name: Transactional modal
examples.code: <Modal open={open} onClose={handleClose} title="Transactional" type="transactional" buttonDefs={{ primary: { onClick: handleSave } }}>Content</Modal>

examples.name: Destructive modal
examples.code: <Modal open={open} onClose={handleClose} title="Destructive" type="destructive" buttonDefs={{ primary: { onClick: handleConfirm } }}>Content</Modal>

examples.name: Feedback modal
examples.code: <Modal open={open} onClose={handleClose} type="feedback" messages={[{ type: 'success', message: 'Success message' }]} />

discouragedProps.name: slots
discouragedProps.reason: Inherited Dialog escape hatch. Use only when replacing MUI internals is required.

discouragedProps.name: style
discouragedProps.reason: Use only for local layout integration.

discouragedProps.name: className
discouragedProps.reason: Do not use to recreate internal HugoUIModal classes.

actions.notes: Primary and secondary render HugoUIButton. Tertiary renders Link unless renderCustomComponent is supplied.

feedback.notes: messages is used when type is feedback and supports success, destructiveSuccess, and error message types.

customRegions.notes: Use only when the standard title and footer do not match the design.

loading.notes: loading constrains action buttons; showLoadingIndicator controls panel-level loading feedback.

generationPolicy: Use type to choose visual intent instead of overriding modal colors.
Do not hardcode header, icon, footer, focus, warning, or error colors.
Use buttonDefs to customize modal actions rather than replacing the footer by default.

a11yRules.rule: Modal requires open to be controlled by the caller.
Provide onClose when the modal should close from Escape, backdrop, close button, or secondary action.
A string title becomes aria-label; a non-string title uses aria-labelledby through ModalTitle.
Feedback messages should provide meaningful message text and optional description.

generationRules.rule: Default generated Modal usage should use type="transactional", a controlled open prop, onClose, title, children, and buttonDefs.primary.
Use buttonDefs.primary for the main action, buttonDefs.secondary for cancel/back, and buttonDefs.tertiary for low-emphasis link actions.
For completion messages, use type="feedback" with messages instead of manually composing a transactional modal.

validationRules.rule: Generated code must import Modal from @hugo-ui/mui.
Generated code must pass open explicitly.
buttonDefs may contain primary, secondary, and tertiary entries matching the ModalFooter public types.

rawContract.needsReviewReason: First contract version captures Modal and ModalFooter public structures, but inherited Dialog props are intentionally not exhaustively recommended.

feedback.notes: messages is used when type is feedback and supports success, destructiveSuccess, and error message types.

customRegions.notes: Use only when the standard title and footer do not match the design.

loading.notes: loading constrains action buttons; showLoadingIndicator controls panel-level loading feedback.

ignoredDesignFields.reason: Design sample content only. Do not hard-code as the runtime Input value.

label.notes: Visible label for normal inputs; search inputs may use the label as placeholder.

values.success: Success status with InputStatus rendering for string extraMessage
values.error: Error status with InputStatus rendering for string extraMessage

multilineCount.notes: showCount applies to multiline inputs and uses inputProps.maxLength or the default max length.

loading.notes: Shows an end adornment loading indicator and sets aria-busy on the HTML input.

ignoredDesignFields.reason: Design sample content only. Do not hard-code as the runtime Input value.

label.notes: Visible label for normal inputs; search inputs may use the label as placeholder.

values.success: Success status with InputStatus rendering for string extraMessage

loading disables click behavior; center loading preserves button width.

iconOnly.notes: Use only when children provides an accessible label and an icon is visible.
